info.name: SecurePoll RW
info.description:
Complete API collection for SecurePoll RW — a biometric voter verification system for Rwanda. Covers authentication, voter registry, biometric enrolment, election-day verification, fraud detection, audit log integrity, and analytics.

Login first with POST /auth/login — the test script auto-sets the `token` collection variable so all subsequent requests are authenticated automatically.

variable.description: Base URL of the SecurePoll RW API server
JWT access token — auto-set by POST /auth/login test script
UUID of a voter — set manually after creating or listing voters
Human-readable voter token used for lookup and verification
Fraud case ID — seeded value for testing
UUID of a duplicate match record
UUID of an audit log entry
Role identifier string (e.g. super, officer, auditor)

item.name: System
item.description: Health checks and server status endpoints. No authentication required.

item.name: Health Check
item.description: Returns {"status": "ok"} when the API is reachable. No authentication required.

exec: pm.test('Response body has status ok', function () {

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication (JWT + TOTP 2FA), session management.

Login flow:
1. POST /auth/login → if mfa_required, go to step 2, otherwise done
2. POST /auth/mfa → full token

item.name: Login
item.description:
Login with email and password. Returns JWT access token. If MFA is enabled, returns a partial token with mfa_required=true — then call POST /auth/mfa.

The test script automatically stores the token as a collection variable.

item.name: Login (OAuth2 Form)
item.description: OAuth2-compatible form login — same as /auth/login but accepts x-www-form-urlencoded body. Used by the /docs Authorize button. Accepts username and password as form fields.

item.name: Verify MFA (TOTP)
item.description: Second step of MFA login. Submit the 6-digit TOTP code from an authenticator app to upgrade a partial token to a full access token.

exec: console.log('Full token stored after MFA verification');

item.name: Refresh Token
item.description: Refresh the current JWT access token. Returns a new token with a fresh expiry. Useful to extend a session without re-entering credentials.

item.name: Logout
item.description: Logout the current user. Since SecurePoll uses stateless JWTs, this is a client-side operation. The server returns a confirmation message and the client should discard the token.

item.name: Users
item.description: Admin user management — list, invite, update, MFA reset, and suspend. All endpoints require authentication.

item.name: List Users
item.description: Retrieve all admin users in the system. Returns full profile including role, status, and last-active timestamp.

item.name: Invite User
item.description: Invite a new admin user by email. The user is created with status `invitation_pending`. Requires super-admin role.

item.name: Get User by ID
item.description: Retrieve a specific admin user by their UUID. Returns full profile.

item.name: Update User
item.description: Update an admin user's full name or district scope. Only the fields provided in the body are updated (partial update).

item.name: Reset User MFA
item.description: Reset MFA for an admin user and generate a new TOTP secret. Returns the raw TOTP secret that must be scanned into an authenticator app.

item.name: Suspend User
item.description: Suspend an admin user account. Suspended users cannot log in. Cannot be used to suspend yourself.

item.name: Roles
item.description: RBAC role management — list, create, and update permission roles.

item.name: List Roles
item.description: Retrieve all RBAC roles defined in the system. Each role has a list of permission strings.

item.name: Create Role
item.description: Create a new RBAC role with a list of permission strings. The role ID must be a unique slug.

item.name: Update Role
item.description: Update an existing role's name and permissions by role ID.

item.name: Sessions
item.description: Active session management for the current authenticated user.

item.name: List My Sessions
item.description: List all active (non-revoked) sessions for the currently authenticated user. Returns device, IP address, location, and last-active time.

item.name: Revoke Session
item.description: Revoke a specific session by UUID. This marks the session as revoked and the associated token becomes invalid.

item.name: Voter Registry
item.description: Voter registry management — create, search, update voter records. Voters progress through statuses: registered → voted; side paths: blocked, flagged, archived.

item.name: List Voters
item.description: Retrieve a paginated list of voters. Supports search by name/token/ref, filter by status, and filter by district.

query.description: Search by name, voter token, or registration ref

item.name: Create Voter
item.description: Create a new voter record in the registry. Validates that the district and polling station exist. Sets status to `registered`.

item.name: Get Voter by Token
item.description: Look up a voter by their human-readable voter token (e.g. RW-001-001-0042). Used by field officers to pull up voter details quickly.

item.name: Get Voter by ID
item.description: Retrieve a voter record by their internal UUID.

item.name: Update Voter
item.description: Update mutable fields on a voter record: first_name, last_name, phone, roll_position. Changes are logged to the audit trail with before/after diffs.

item.name: Block Voter
item.description: Block a voter with a mandatory reason. Blocked voters cannot be verified at the polling station. Sets status to `blocked` and logs the action.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/voters/{{voter_id}}:block?reason=Suspected identity fraud&note=Biometric match with flagged record

item.name: Archive Voter
item.description: Archive a voter record. Sets status to `archived`. Archived records are retained for audit purposes but excluded from active verification.

item.name: Restore Voter
item.description: Restore a blocked or archived voter back to `registered` status.

item.name: Export Voters (CSV)
item.description: Export the full voter registry as a CSV file. Returns a streaming CSV download with headers: voter_token, registration_ref, first_name, last_name, sex, date_of_birth, status, district_id, polling_station_id.

item.name: Import Voters (CSV)
item.description: Bulk-import voters from a CSV file. Expected columns: voter_token, registration_ref, national_id, first_name, last_name, sex, date_of_birth, phone, district_id, polling_station_id. Returns count of added, flagged, and rejected rows.

formdata.description: CSV file to import. Must have headers: voter_token,registration_ref,national_id,first_name,last_name,sex,date_of_birth,phone,district_id,polling_station_id

item.name: Registry Health
item.description: Get a health summary of the voter registry: total voters, breakdown by status (registered, voted, flagged, blocked), and data quality indicator.

exec: pm.test('Registry health has expected fields', function () {

item.name: Biometrics
item.description: Face biometric enrolment using ArcFace 512-d embeddings with AES-256-GCM encryption at rest. A 1:N FAISS deduplication scan runs automatically at enrolment.

item.name: Enroll Face Biometric
item.description: Enrol a voter's face biometric. Accepts a multipart form with voter_id and face_image (base64-encoded JPEG/PNG). Runs liveness detection, quality assessment, and 1:N deduplication check. Creates a fraud case if a near-duplicate is found.

exec: pm.test('Enrollment response has quality score', function () {

formdata.description: UUID of the voter to enrol
Base64-encoded face image (JPEG or PNG). Replace with real base64 data.

item.name: Get Biometric Quality
item.description: Retrieve the biometric quality score and enrollment metadata for a specific voter. Returns quality score, liveness result, and capture timestamp.

item.name: Verification
item.description: Election-day 1:1 face verification and double-vote-protected vote casting. The verify endpoint is public (no JWT required) so field officers can call it directly from polling station devices.

request.description: Retrieve a single verification attempt by its UUID.

item.name: Verify Voter (1:1 Face Match)
item.description: Perform a 1:1 biometric verification against a voter's enrolled template. Public endpoint — no JWT required. Accepts voter_token, polling_station_id, officer_id, and face_image. Returns a decision panel with confidence score, liveness result, flags, and explainability breakdown.

exec: pm.test('Verification response has result field', function () {

item.name: List Verification Attempts
item.description: List all verification attempts. Returns a paginated list with result, confidence, liveness, and flags for each attempt.

item.name: Get Verification Attempt
item.description: Retrieve a specific verification attempt by UUID. Returns full decision panel including confidence, liveness, flags, and AI explainability breakdown.

item.name: Override Verification Decision
item.description: Manually override the AI decision on a verification attempt. Used when a supervisor reviews a case flagged for manual review and decides to approve or reject.

item.name: Station Verification Log
item.description: Retrieve the verification log for a specific polling station. Returns all verification attempts processed at that station, useful for station-level monitoring.

item.name: Votes
item.description: Double-vote-protected vote casting. Uses a PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE row-lock to prevent concurrent duplicate votes.

item.name: Cast Vote
item.description: Record that a voter has voted. Uses a PostgreSQL row-lock to prevent concurrent double-votes. Requires voter_id, officer_id, and polling_station_id. Updates voter status to `voted` atomically.

exec: pm.test('Vote cast response has status voted', function () {

item.name: Fraud Cases
item.description: Fraud cases auto-created by the biometric enrolment pipeline when a 1:N match exceeds the similarity threshold. Operators can dismiss or escalate cases.

item.name: List Fraud Cases
item.description: Retrieve all fraud cases. Returns case ID, type, risk level, score, verdict, and linked voter/station details.

item.name: Get Fraud Case
item.description: Retrieve a specific fraud case by ID. Returns full case details including breakdown, timeline, assessment, and similarity score.

item.name: Dismiss Fraud Case
item.description: Dismiss a fraud case, marking it as a false positive. Requires a resolution note. Sets resolution to `dismissed`.

item.name: Escalate Fraud Case
item.description: Escalate a fraud case to a higher authority. Requires a reason. Sets risk level to high and flags the case for senior review.

item.name: Fraud Summary
item.description: Get an aggregated summary of fraud cases: counts by type, risk level distribution, open vs. resolved counts.

item.name: Duplicates
item.description: Biometric 1:N duplicate match management. Duplicates are auto-detected at enrolment time via FAISS similarity search. Operators can merge duplicate voter records.

request.description: Retrieve a single duplicate match by its UUID.

item.name: List Duplicate Matches
item.description: List all biometric duplicate matches. Each record has a similarity score and status (pending, merged, dismissed).

item.name: Get Duplicate Match
item.description: Retrieve details of a specific duplicate match by UUID. Returns both voter records (A and B), similarity score, and resolution status.

item.name: Merge Duplicate Records
item.description: Merge two duplicate voter records. The `survivor_id` specifies which record to keep. The other record is archived. Sets match status to `merged`.

item.name: Anomaly Signals
item.description: Real-time anomaly signal management. Signals are generated by the AI anomaly detection pipeline when unusual patterns are detected (e.g., velocity spikes, geographic impossibilities).

request.description: Retrieve a single anomaly signal by its string ID.

item.name: List Anomaly Signals
item.description: List all anomaly signals. Returns signal ID, type, severity, description, and acknowledgement status.

item.name: Get Anomaly Signal
item.description: Retrieve details of a specific anomaly signal by ID.

item.name: Acknowledge Anomaly Signal
item.description: Acknowledge an anomaly signal to mark it as reviewed. Requires a note explaining the review decision.

item.name: Mute Anomaly Signal
item.description: Mute an anomaly signal to suppress future notifications of the same type. Useful for known, benign patterns.

item.name: Audit Log
item.description: Append-only SHA-256 hash-chained audit log. Every write operation across the system produces an immutable audit entry. The verify-chain endpoint walks the full chain and reports any tampered rows.

item.name: List Audit Entries
item.description: Retrieve a paginated list of audit log entries. Each entry includes the action, actor, service, timestamp, and hash-chain values.

item.name: Get Audit Entry
item.description: Retrieve a specific audit log entry by UUID. Returns the full entry including hash values for chain verification.

exec: pm.test('Audit entry has hash fields', function () {

item.name: Verify Audit Chain Integrity
item.description: Walk the full SHA-256 hash chain and verify every entry. Reports the number of entries verified, any breaks found, the sequence number of the first break, and the duration. A healthy chain has breaks_found == 0.

exec: pm.test('Chain verification has required fields', function () {

item.name: Export Audit Log
item.description: Export the full audit log as a downloadable file (CSV or JSON). Useful for compliance reporting and external audits.

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Real-time turnout statistics, demographic breakdown by sex and district, and verification performance metrics.

item.name: Turnout Statistics
item.description: Get real-time election turnout statistics. Returns total registered voters, total verified, total voted, and a breakdown by polling station.

exec: pm.test('Turnout stats has required fields', function () {

item.name: Demographics Breakdown
item.description: Get demographic breakdown of verified voters by sex and district. Returns counts and percentages.

item.name: Verification Statistics
item.description: Get verification performance metrics: total attempts, approval rate, manual review rate, rejection rate, and average confidence score.

exec: pm.test('Verification stats has required fields', function () {
